scores.author: Travis Jaudon
scores.title: Tybee City Manager search close to wrapping, three finalists to be interviewed next week
scores.rationale: Routine governance progress (filling a vacancy) is mildly positive; reporting is straight and balanced, quoting the mayor without editorial slant.

scores.author: Travis Jaudon
scores.title: Future of Civic Center site likely decided by Savannah City Council this week
scores.rationale: Roundup of routine civic news—council decision, July 4 fireworks, and mixed crime stats (homicides up, gun assaults down)—reported in straightforward, neutral fashion.

scores.author: Chantel Britton
scores.title: Community News Connection
scores.rationale: Compilation of positive community events—partnerships, free classes, workshops, and a legislative appointment—written in promotional, upbeat press-release style favorable to its subjects.

scores.author: Frank Ricci
scores.title: THE CONNECT FIVE: Shows to see this week in Savannah
scores.rationale: Upbeat weekly concert guide celebrating live music with enthusiastic, promotional framing praising each act, yielding positive valence and boosterish tone.

scores.author: Beth Logan
scores.title: Canadian Couple Create Art that’s Accessible to All
scores.rationale: Uplifting feature on accessible art celebrates inclusion and the artists' work; warm, admiring framing ('quite simply, beautiful') gives both positive valence and favorable tone.

scores.author: Travis Jaudon
scores.title: Leadership changes on July 1 mean start of new era for GHSA
scores.rationale: Routine leadership-change announcement framed positively with optimistic quotes about new hires, yielding mildly positive valence and a near-neutral, slightly favorable tone.

scores.author: Eric Curl
scores.title: Property Matters: First homes built, sold as part of Cuyler-Brownville housing initiative
scores.rationale: Positive story of first-time homeowner and successful affordable housing development; mostly factual reporting with a warm, lightly favorable framing around the homecoming.

scores.author: Travis Jaudon
scores.title: What Savannah Mayor would ask Biden, Trump if he were moderating Thursday’s CNN Presidential Debate
scores.rationale: Light feature on mayor's debate views; mildly positive due to call for civility, reported neutrally with minimal editorial slant.

scores.author: Chantel Britton
scores.title: Breaking Good News: Nonprofit Highlights
scores.rationale: Explicitly upbeat 'good news' roundup of volunteer opportunities, charity drives, and grants; promotional, celebratory framing yields strongly positive valence and favorable tone.

scores.author: Connect Savannah Staff
scores.title: Savannah State University and HCA Healthcare Begin Major Strategic Partnership with Paid Internship Program
scores.rationale: Positive announcement of funding and paid internships; promotional framing with celebratory quotes and institutional praise pushes tone favorable.

scores.author: Neil Gabbey
scores.title: Overflow Coffee sets up Shoppe in Skidaway Community Church’s parking lot
scores.rationale: Uplifting feature on a charity coffee house; subject is positive and the writing is warmly promotional, praising the mission as 'wonderful.'

scores.author: Travis Jaudon
scores.title: Dr. Denise Watts reflects on personal growth in first year as SCCPSS Superintendent
scores.rationale: Upbeat retrospective on accomplishments and growth gives positive valence; reporting largely relays the superintendent's favorable self-assessment with little critical balance, tilting tone mildly positive.

scores.author: Travis Jaudon
scores.title: Tybee City Manager job will be filled by one of these three finalists
scores.rationale: Routine process story with controversy over open-records compliance and council disagreement; reported in balanced fashion quoting all sides, leaving valence slightly negative, tone neutral.

scores.author: Chantel Britton

scores.author: Travis Jaudon
scores.title: Search Committee of 12 tasked with finding Savannah State’s next president
scores.rationale: Straight procedural reporting on a presidential search committee; note of leadership turnover (four presidents in five years) tempers the mildly positive institutional framing, with neutral objective tone.

scores.author: Brett Bigelow
scores.title: Victory North: Savannah’s Musical Oasis Celebrates Five Years
